titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( (Winant))

div.head: The Secretary of State to the Ambassador in the United Kingdom ( Winant )
div.p: 7143. For Collado from Reinstein.Jacques J. Reinstein, Associate Chief, Division of Financial Affairs. Have cabled separately text of letters exchanged by Acting Secretary and Representative CellerSee footnote 50, p. 80. on possible credit to UK. Department’s letter drafted by Earley and sent out inadvertently without either Phelps or me having approved it.

There are certain parts of letter which Phelps and I thought should be rewritten or eliminated. I particularly want draw your attention last sentence of letter which suggests that granting of any credit should be tied up with relaxation of British wartime restrictions, but makes no reference to trade restrictions practiced before war.In reply, in telegram 8676, August 25, 4 p.m., from London, Collado stated: “Our conversations with the British Treasury have taken the line that both wartime and other trade discriminations must be removed.” (841.51/8–2545)
In view of publicity which letter has received it seemed desirable to inform you of circumstances under which it was sent. [Reinstein.]

postscript.p: [On August 24, Foreign Economic Administrator Leo T. Crowley issued a supplementary statement on the termination of lend-lease, outlining a transitional program; for text, see New York Times, August 25, 1945, page 5, column 2.]
